Schweitzer SEL-701 Motor Protection Relay
Instruction Manual: Date Code 20010719
NOTE:
Both device functions '49' and '49 Sec Amps' represent the same 
thermal overload function. If you use '49', EasyPower FLA data is 
entered in Primary Amps, whereas FLA data entered in the actual
relay is secondary FLA. The pickup is for adjusting the service factor.
If you use '49 Sec Amps function', enter secondary FLA in Pickup 
settings. The Service factor is not modeled in 49 Sec FLA.

Device functions '49/50'' 
The pickup is CT ratio * amps setting*service factor
For example with motor FLA = 140, CT 250/5, & SF=1.15
Setting = 140/(250/5) = 2.8A, Setting window shows 140A pickup 
On the TCC the 701 the curve  wiill go to 140*115=161A

Toshiba RC820 (2E Relay)
Motor Protection

Instruction Manual : 6F9E0092

NOTE:
The internal CT of 7:1 or 55:1 or 110:1 have not been 
included in relay model in library.  Create a CT in the
one-line diagram to represent the internal CT or Ampere-turns
rating of the relay.
If an external CT is used, multiply the ratio by the internal CT rating.

Under-voltage (27) and over-voltage (59) relays use CT as input.  
The CT primary and secondary amps represent PT primary 
line-to-line voltage (in volts) and secondary volts.   
For example, if an under-voltage relay (27) at the 13.8 kV system
is set at 100V with 14400V/120V PT, enter 14400 and 120 
(or 120 and 1) as CT primary and secondary amps respectively,
and set 100V for function 27.
